docs.text: A curated set of quick, nourishing recipes designed for busy days.

docs.title: Browse recipes
docs.text: -   :material-blender: **[Frozen Triple Berry Smoothie](frozen-triple-berry-smoothie.md)**      Thick, creamy, and ready in minutes — perfect for breakfast or a snack.  -   :material-bowl-mix: **[Garlic Sautéed Red Rice Bowl](garlic-sauteed-red-rice.md)**      A build-your-own bowl with a garlicky rice base, roasted veg, and a protein of choice.

docs.title: Recipe flow at a glance
docs.text:
1. Pick your base → choose the recipe that fits your time and mood.
  2. Gather ingredients → review the quick prep notes and pantry swaps.
  3. Cook or blend → follow the step-by-step flow for consistent results.
  4. Finish + enjoy → garnish, serve, and save leftovers for later.

docs.title: Frozen Triple Berry Smoothie
docs.text: A single-serving smoothie with a thick, creamy texture that works well for breakfast or a quick snack.

docs.title: Quick facts
docs.text: Prep time 5 minutes Yield 1 serving Texture goal Thick, spoonable, and ice-cream-like

docs.title: Ingredients
docs.text:
Ingredient Quantity Notes Frozen triple berry blend 80 g Strawberries, blueberries, raspberries, or your preferred mix. Frozen banana 1/2 banana Peel, chop, and freeze ahead of time for the best texture. Greek yogurt (plain) 1/2 cup (115 g) Use full-fat or 2% for maximum creaminess. Milk 1/4 to 1/2 cup Adjust based on desired thickness. Start with less and add more. Honey 1 tablespoon Optional sweetener.

Make-ahead prep

Portion the frozen fruit and banana into a freezer bag so it is ready to blend.

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Load the blender. Add the frozen berries, frozen banana, and Greek yogurt to the blender jar.
  2. Add liquid. Pour in 1/4 cup milk. Add honey if using.
  3. Blend slowly. Start on low speed, then increase to high until the mixture begins to move.
  4. Scrape and adjust. Stop the blender, scrape down the sides, and use a tamper if available.
  5. Dial in texture. Add the remaining milk one tablespoon at a time until the smoothie is thick but moves freely.
  6. Serve immediately. Pour into a glass or bowl and enjoy.

docs.title: Serving ideas
docs.text:
• Add a sprinkle of granola for crunch.
  • Top with extra berries or chia seeds.

docs.title: Garlic Sautéed Red Rice Bowl
docs.text: A warm, customizable bowl with a garlicky red rice base, roasted veggies, and a protein of your choice.

docs.title: Quick facts
docs.text: Prep time 15 minutes Cook time 25 minutes Yield 1 serving

docs.title: Recipe flow
docs.text:
1. Cook the red rice base.
  2. Roast the vegetables while the rice cooks.
  3. Choose a protein and cook it.
  4. Whisk the sauce and assemble the bowl.

docs.title: Ingredients
docs.text: Ingredient Quantity Notes Red rice (dry) 70 g Water 120–130 ml Garlic 2 cloves Minced Button mushrooms 80 g Sliced Egg 1 Oil 1 tbsp Seasoning To taste Salt + black pepper Mixed herbs 1/2 tsp Optional

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Cook rice. Rinse red rice, cook with water, then fluff and rest for 5 minutes.
  2. Sauté aromatics. In a medium pan, heat oil, sauté garlic for 10 seconds, then mushrooms for 2–3 minutes.
  3. Scramble egg. Push the veggies aside and scramble the egg in the same pan.
  4. Stir-fry. Add cooked rice, seasoning, and mixed herbs (if using). Stir-fry 2–3 minutes.

docs.title: Ingredients & prep
docs.text: Ingredient Quantity Prep Broccoli 150 g Season with 1 clove minced garlic, 1 tsp oil, salt & pepper. Mushrooms 80–120 g Season with 1 tsp oil, salt & pepper. Corn 70 g Steam or microwave for 2–3 minutes if frozen.

docs.title: Roast instructions
docs.text:
1. Preheat oven to 200°C.
  2. Roast broccoli and mushrooms for 12–15 minutes, flipping halfway through.

docs.title: C) Protein (Pick 1)
docs.text:
Roasted Chicken Breast (~90 g cooked)Garlic Tiger Prawns (~60 g cooked)

Ingredients

Ingredient Quantity Notes Chicken breast (raw) 130 g Garlic 1 clove Minced Oil 1 tsp Seasoning To taste Salt + pepper (optional paprika)

Steps

  1. Rub the chicken with oil, garlic, salt, and pepper/paprika.
  2. Roast at 200°C for 15–18 minutes (same oven as the vegetables).
  3. Rest 5 minutes, then slice.

Ingredients

Ingredient Quantity Notes Tiger prawns (raw) 90 g Peeled Garlic 1 clove Minced Oil (or butter) 1 tsp Seasoning To taste Salt + pepper (optional lemon)

Steps

  1. Pat prawns dry.
  2. In a medium pan, heat oil/butter, sauté garlic for 10 seconds.
  3. Cook prawns 1.5–2 minutes per side until pink.

docs.title: Ingredients
docs.text: Ingredient Quantity Notes Lemon juice 1 tbsp (15 ml) Light soy sauce 1 tbsp (15 ml) Olive oil 1 tsp (5 ml) Honey 1/2 tsp Garlic 1 clove Minced Black pepper To taste

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Whisk all ingredients together until the honey dissolves.
  2. Drizzle over the assembled bowl just before serving.

docs.title: Assemble the bowl
docs.text:
1. Spoon the red rice into a bowl.
  2. Add roasted vegetables, corn, and your chosen protein.
  3. Finish with sauce and extra herbs if desired.
